metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Male
Competitive Agreeableness (29th pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Low C Conscientiousness (35th pct) — Flexible, spontaneous, disorganised
Extraverted Extraversion (76th pct) — Extraverted, outgoing, energetic
Stable Neuroticism (25th pct) — Emotionally stable, calm, resilient
Highly open Openness to Experience (91st pct) — Open, curious, imaginative, creative
  Low Tender-Mindedness (34th pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Moderate Achievement Striving (55th pct)
  Low Self-Discipline (38th pct) — Procrastinates, easily distracted
  High Activity Level (71st pct) — Fast-paced, energetic, busy
  High Excitement Seeking (67th pct) — Thrill-seeking, excitement-hungry
  High Positive Emotions (63rd pct) — Joyful, enthusiastic, optimistic
  Low Anger / Hostility (25th pct) — Slow to anger, easy-going
  Low Self-Consciousness (21st pct) — Comfortable socially, not easily embarrassed
  Stable Neuroticism (21st pct) — Emotionally stable, calm, resilient
  Moderate Chronological Age (55th pct)

message.reasoning:
We are simulating a baseline opinion on the policy item "Trust In Mainstream Journalism". The profile is a 55-year-old male with high openness (91st pct), high extraversion (76th pct), low agreeableness (29th pct) especially low trust (18th pct), low conscientiousness (35th pct) especially low deliberation (19th pct) and low order (16th pct), low neuroticism (25th pct). He is emotionally stable, outgoing, highly open to new ideas and experiences, but skeptical and not trusting. He is also low on tender-mindedness (34th pct) meaning tough-minded.

Given the low trust facet of agreeableness (18th pct) and low straightforwardness (32nd pct), combined with high openness to values and ideas, he is likely to be skeptical of mainstream institutions including journalism. He is not compliant (27.5th pct) and is independent-minded. His high extraversion suggests he engages with information socially, but his low trust and high openness make him likely to question mainstream narratives. He might be drawn to alternative sources. Also, low deliberation means he may not carefully scrutinize information but his skepticism suggests he distrusts mainstream journalism. However, he is not extremely angry or hostile, so his opposition is not aggressive but more of a reasoned skepticism. Given that he is highly open, he might appreciate diverse viewpoints but his low trust makes him doubt the reliability of mainstream sources. He is also older (55), which correlates with slightly higher trust in some studies, but his personality traits dominate. Overall, I expect a moderate to strong distrust. But not maximal because he is stable and not deeply entrenched. Also, low neuroticism means he is not anxious about media, so his distrust is calm and deliberate. Let's aim for around -500 to -600. But need to avoid round numbers. Let's say -537. Confidence: moderate, because trust is multifaceted.
